claims.claim: Under moderate sparsity (top-4 of 16, density 0.25), all schedules converge to perfect accuracy, rendering the task insufficiently discriminative.

claims.claim: Under extreme sparsity (top-1 of 16, density 0.0625) with a limited training budget (120 steps), sparse-from-start training fails near chance level (mean accuracy 0.105 ± 0.022), while every dense-warm-start variant—linear curriculum, cubic curriculum, and hard 50% cutover—achieves perfect target-sparse accuracy (1.000 ± 0.000).

claims.claim: Our results confirm that dense warm-start dramatically improves trainability under extreme sparsity, but we find no evidence that gradual annealing outperforms simpler alternatives.
